title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 3 (Book 2)
source: Practice Tests CPE 2, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-18, read the three texts below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
The Rothschilds

When, in the 18th century, Mayer Anscher Rothschild (1) ______ his studies to join a banking firm in his native Frankfurt, he took the first steps towards creating one of the most successful, and most influential banks.

For nearly two centuries, the fortunes of the British Government and those of the bank were fundamentally (2) ______. Thanks to the Rothschilds, the Duke of Wellington was able to pay his army in 1814-15 (the bank received a two per cent commission on the deal). Ten years later, the Rothschilds again came to the (3) ______ when 145 British banks failed, helping prevent the (4) ______ of the whole UK banking system.

The 19th century Prime Minister, Benjamin Disraeli (5) ______ one of his great coups as buying enough shares in the Suez Canal to secure Britain a controlling interest — again thanks to a £4 million Rothschild loan. Today, the company (6) ______ among the world's largest privately-owned banks, with 40 offices in 30 different countries.

Akenfield

Down by the river lie the currant and gooseberry bushes — literally the fruit of the potter's field — for the loam there is (7) ______ with Roman pottery. Just above, the bit of straight — or the army path as the Saxon farmers called it — (8) ______ past towards the coast.

The heights are crowned with mill sites and within the village proper there exists an empty secondary horse village, a deserted (9) ______ of packways, stables, harness rooms and tackle. Nothing has contributed more to the swift destruction of the old pattern of life in Suffolk than the death of the horse. It (10) ______ with it a quite different conception of time.

The old farmsteads ride high on the hills. They must remain remote unless some huge (11) ______ project thrusts up to meet them. And this is not likely. Akenfield itself has no development plans and even if Ipswich's overspill ever threatened it, it is doubtful if any preservationist society would launch an (12) ______ to save it. It is not that kind of village.

Nothing But Style

Style is now more important than substance or at least more important than skills; that is the message of a report (13) ______ last month in Britain that questions the (14) ______ employees project and how it compares to the one a would-be employer wants to promote.

It is now more important to employers that their employees (15) ______ the lifestyle being sold by the restaurant, cafe or shop in which they work, than have technical skills. The idea that workers personify a company has long been an (16) ______ part of management-level jobs, but this concept has (17) ______ to jobs such as shop assistants and waiters.

Employers now rely more on appearance and accent than on qualifications. So is getting a job all a (18) ______ of style? At many coffee houses and restaurant chains, the answer would seem to be yes.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'abandoned his studies' = bỏ dở việc học. 'Abandon' dùng để chỉ việc từ bỏ một hoạt động, kế hoạch. Bẫy: 'abdicated' (A) nghĩa là thoái vị (dùng cho vua chúa), không phù hợp với 'studies'. 'absconded' (C) nghĩa là bỏ trốn (thường khỏi pháp luật). 'discarded' (D) nghĩa là vứt bỏ (đồ vật), không phù hợp với việc học. Chuyên đề: Collocations — abandon + studies/plans.
questions.answerLocation: abandoned his studies to join a banking firm

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'fundamentally linked' = gắn kết cơ bản. 'Linked' dùng với nghĩa trừu tượng để chỉ mối liên hệ giữa hai thực thể. Bẫy: 'bound' (A) cần giới từ 'to' hoặc 'up with', không đứng một mình. 'fastened' (B) mang nghĩa vật lý (buộc chặt). 'bonded' (C) thường dùng với nghĩa hóa học hoặc tình cảm cá nhân, không phù hợp ngữ cảnh tài chính. Chuyên đề: Collocations — linked/bound.
questions.answerLocation: fortunes were fundamentally linked

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'came to the rescue' = đến giải cứu/cứu trợ. Đây là collocation cố định. Bẫy: 'came to the deliverance' (A) không phải collocation chuẩn. 'salvation' (C) mang nghĩa tôn giáo, không tạo thành cụm 'come to the salvation'. 'relief' (D) không đi với 'come to the'. Chuyên đề: Collocations — come to the rescue.
questions.answerLocation: came to the rescue when 145 British banks failed

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'the collapse of the banking system' = sự sụp đổ của hệ thống ngân hàng. 'Collapse' là từ chuẩn dùng với hệ thống, tổ chức, nền kinh tế. Bẫy: 'subsidence' (A) nghĩa là sụt lún (đất đai). 'descent' (B) nghĩa là sự đi xuống/suy thoái nhưng không mạnh bằng 'collapse'. 'demolition' (D) nghĩa là phá hủy (có chủ đích), không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Collocations — collapse of a system.
questions.answerLocation: prevent the collapse of the whole UK banking system

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'saw one of his great coups as...' = coi/xem một trong những chiến công lớn nhất của mình là... 'See something as' = coi cái gì đó là. Bẫy: 'observed' (A) nghĩa là quan sát, không dùng với cấu trúc 'observe sth as doing'. 'beheld' (B) là quá khứ của 'behold' (chiêm ngưỡng), rất trang trọng. 'watched' (D) nghĩa là theo dõi, không phù hợp cấu trúc. Chuyên đề: Verbs of perception — see as.
questions.answerLocation: Disraeli saw one of his great coups as buying

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'ranks among' = xếp hạng trong số/nằm trong hàng ngũ. 'The company ranks among the world's largest banks' = công ty xếp hạng trong số những ngân hàng lớn nhất. Bẫy: 'classes' (B) không dùng như động từ trong ngữ cảnh này. 'ranges' (C) cần 'from...to', không đi với 'among'. 'positions' (D) không dùng như động từ intransitive với 'among'. Chuyên đề: Collocations — rank among.
questions.answerLocation: the company ranks among the world's largest

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'littered with' = rải rác đầy, phủ đầy. 'The loam is littered with Roman pottery' = đất sét rải rác đầy đồ gốm La Mã. 'Littered with' là collocation chuẩn cho bề mặt có nhiều vật rải rác. Bẫy: 'splashed' (A) dùng với chất lỏng. 'spattered' (B) cũng dùng với chất lỏng (bắn tung tóe). 'dispersed' (D) nghĩa là phân tán, thường dùng cho đám đông. Chuyên đề: Collocations — littered with.
questions.answerLocation: loam there is littered with Roman pottery

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'shoots past' = lao qua nhanh. 'The path shoots past towards the coast' = con đường vút qua hướng về phía biển. 'Shoot past' tạo hình ảnh sinh động về con đường thẳng chạy nhanh. Bẫy: 'bolts' (B) thường dùng cho người/động vật bỏ chạy. 'dashes' (C) thường dùng cho người. 'hurls' (D) nghĩa là ném mạnh, không phù hợp cho con đường. Chuyên đề: Collocations — shoot past (movement).
questions.answerLocation: the army path shoots past towards the coast

questions.instruction: Gap (9):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'a deserted complex of packways, stables...' = một khu phức hợp bị bỏ hoang gồm đường chở hàng, chuồng ngựa... 'Complex' là danh từ chỉ một nhóm các tòa nhà/cơ sở có liên quan với nhau. Bẫy: 'scheme' (A) nghĩa là kế hoạch/đề án, không phù hợp ngữ cảnh vật lý. 'collectivity' (B) là từ trừu tượng. 'entirety' (C) nghĩa là toàn bộ, không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — complex (noun).
questions.answerLocation: a deserted complex of packways, stables

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'It carried away with it a quite different conception of time' = Nó mang đi theo một quan niệm hoàn toàn khác về thời gian. 'Carry away' = mang đi, cuốn đi. Cái chết của ngựa đã mang đi một cách nhìn khác về thời gian. Bẫy: 'drew away' (A) nghĩa là rút lui/rời đi. 'made away' (C) cần 'with' và nghĩa là trộm cắp hoặc giết. 'ran away' (D) nghĩa là bỏ chạy. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — carry away. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: It carried away with it a quite different conception of time

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'housing project' = dự án nhà ở. Đây là collocation cố định trong tiếng Anh. 'A huge housing project' = một dự án nhà ở lớn. Bẫy: 'sheltering project' (B) không phải collocation chuẩn. 'accommodating project' (C) không phải collocation. 'dwelling project' (D) cũng không phải collocation chuẩn — 'dwelling' thường đứng một mình hoặc trong 'dwelling place'. Chuyên đề: Collocations — housing project.
questions.answerLocation: some huge housing project thrusts up

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'launch an appeal' = phát động lời kêu gọi. 'Launch an appeal to save it' = phát động lời kêu gọi bảo tồn nó. Đây là collocation chuẩn, đặc biệt trong ngữ cảnh bảo tồn di sản. Bẫy: 'launch an attack' (A) quá mạnh và mang nghĩa tiêu cực. 'launch an effort' (B) không tự nhiên — nên dùng 'make an effort'. 'launch an order' (D) không phải collocation chuẩn. Chuyên đề: Collocations — launch an appeal.
questions.answerLocation: launch an appeal to save it

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'brought out' = xuất bản, phát hành. 'A report brought out last month' = một báo cáo được phát hành tháng trước. 'Bring out' là phrasal verb nghĩa là xuất bản/công bố. Bẫy: 'taken out' (A) nghĩa là rút ra/mang ra. 'caught out' (B) nghĩa là bị phát hiện sai. 'worked out' (C) nghĩa là giải quyết/tập thể dục. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — bring out. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: a report brought out last month

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'the image employees project' = hình ảnh mà nhân viên thể hiện. 'Image' là từ phù hợp nhất khi nói về cách một người thể hiện bản thân ra bên ngoài, đặc biệt trong bối cảnh kinh doanh. Bẫy: 'impression' (A) nghĩa là ấn tượng, nhưng 'project an image' là collocation chuẩn hơn 'project an impression'. 'picture' (C) quá cụ thể. 'notion' (D) nghĩa là khái niệm, không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Collocations — project an image.
questions.answerLocation: the image employees project

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'reflect the lifestyle' = phản ánh/thể hiện phong cách sống. 'Employees reflect the lifestyle being sold' = nhân viên phản ánh phong cách sống mà cửa hàng đang bán. Bẫy: 'expose' (B) nghĩa là phơi bày (thường mang nghĩa tiêu cực). 'feature' (C) nghĩa là nổi bật/giới thiệu, không phù hợp ngữ pháp. 'flaunt' (D) nghĩa là phô trương một cách khoe khoang. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — reflect.
questions.answerLocation: employees reflect the lifestyle being sold

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'an accepted part of' = một phần được chấp nhận/công nhận. 'Has long been an accepted part of management-level jobs' = từ lâu đã là phần được thừa nhận của công việc quản lý. Bẫy: 'exceptional' (A) nghĩa là ngoại lệ/xuất sắc, không phù hợp ngữ cảnh (đang nói về điều bình thường). 'expressed' (C) nghĩa là được thể hiện, không phù hợp. 'observed' (D) nghĩa là được quan sát, không tự nhiên. Chuyên đề: Collocations — accepted part.
questions.answerLocation: an accepted part of management-level jobs

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'filtered down to' = lan truyền xuống/truyền dần xuống cấp dưới. 'This concept has filtered down to jobs such as shop assistants' = khái niệm này đã lan truyền xuống các công việc như nhân viên bán hàng. 'Filter down' là phrasal verb chuẩn chỉ sự lan truyền từ cấp cao xuống cấp thấp. Bẫy: 'soaked down' (A) không phải phrasal verb chuẩn. 'dripped down' (B) nghĩa đen là nhỏ giọt. 'leaked down' (D) cũng không phải phrasal verb chuẩn — 'leak out' thì có. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — filter down.
questions.answerLocation: concept has filtered down to jobs

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'a matter of style' = vấn đề về phong cách. 'Is getting a job all a matter of style?' = việc xin việc có phải hoàn toàn là vấn đề phong cách? 'A matter of' là cấu trúc cố định rất phổ biến. Bẫy: 'a business of' (A) không phải collocation chuẩn. 'a subject of' (B) nghĩa là chủ đề/đề tài, không phù hợp. 'a concern of' (C) nghĩa là mối lo ngại, không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — a matter of.
questions.answerLocation: all a matter of style

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-15, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each space. Use only one word in each space.
sections.passageHtml:
Bazaars

A bazaar was originally a public market district (0) OF a Persian town. From Persia the term spread to Arabia, Turkey, and North Africa. In India it came to (1) ______ applied to a single shop; and in current English usage it refers to (2) ______ a single shop or concession selling miscellaneous articles and to a fair at (3) ______ such miscellany is sold, often for charity.

The familiar bazaar of the ancient Islamic nations is vividly described (4) ______ the traditional folktales of 'The Thousand and One Nights'. (5) ______ is a distinct quarter of the town, access (6) ______ which is forbidden after sundown, bustling and noisy by day, (7) ______ the quieter residential quarters.

Such a bazaar may be divided into districts, (8) ______ all the purveyors of one type of merchandise grouped together. (9) ______ the bazaar in smaller towns is (10) ______ of a single narrow street of stalls, in larger cities such as Istanbul it is by (11) ______ means simple, consisting of many miles of such passageways.

Some bazaars, such as the (12) ______ built at Sashan and Isfahan in Iran in the 17th century, were designed with great architectural integrity. They were usually roofed for protection (13) ______ the hot desert sun, (14) ______ with a single roof of individual vaulted domes or with awnings. Most of these ancient bazaars have gradually been modernized (15) ______ the centuries.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (1): it came to ______ applied to a single shop
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BE. 'came to be applied' = dần được áp dụng/sử dụng. 'Come to be' + past participle là cấu trúc chỉ sự phát triển dần dần theo thời gian. Chuyên đề: Grammar — come to be + past participle.
questions.answerLocation: it came to be applied to a single shop

questions.instruction: Gap (2): it refers to ______ a single shop or concession
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EITHER. 'either...and' hoặc 'either...or' — ở đây 'it refers to either a single shop or concession... and to a fair at which...' Cấu trúc 'either A or/and B' dùng để liệt kê hai khả năng. Chuyên đề: Conjunctions — either...or.
questions.answerLocation: refers to either a single shop or concession

questions.instruction: Gap (3): a fair at ______ such miscellany is sold
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHICH. 'at which' = tại đó. Đại từ quan hệ 'which' đi sau giới từ 'at' để chỉ 'a fair'. 'A fair at which such miscellany is sold' = một hội chợ mà tại đó những hàng hóa đa dạng được bán. Chuyên đề: Relative pronouns — preposition + which.
questions.answerLocation: a fair at which such miscellany is sold

questions.instruction: Gap (4): vividly described ______ the traditional folktales
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IN. 'described in' = được mô tả trong. 'Vividly described in the traditional folktales' = được mô tả sống động trong các câu chuyện dân gian. Giới từ 'in' dùng với tác phẩm văn học, sách, truyện. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — described in.
questions.answerLocation: described in the traditional folktales

questions.instruction: Gap (5): ______ is a distinct quarter of the town
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IT. 'It is a distinct quarter' = Nó là một khu vực riêng biệt. 'It' ở đây chỉ 'the familiar bazaar' đã được đề cập trước đó. Chuyên đề: Reference pronouns — it. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: It is a distinct quarter of the town

questions.instruction: Gap (6): access ______ which is forbidden after sundown
questions.explanation: Đáp án: TO. 'access to which' = sự tiếp cận/truy cập vào đó. 'Access to' là collocation cố định. 'Access to which is forbidden after sundown' = sự ra vào bị cấm sau khi mặt trời lặn. Chuyên đề: Collocations — access to + relative clause.
questions.answerLocation: access to which is forbidden after sundown

questions.instruction: Gap (7): bustling and noisy by day, ______ the quieter residential quarters
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNLIKE. 'unlike the quieter residential quarters' = không giống những khu dân cư yên tĩnh hơn. 'Unlike' tạo sự đối lập giữa bazaar (bustling/noisy) và khu dân cư (quieter). Chuyên đề: Prepositions — unlike (contrast).
questions.answerLocation: bustling and noisy by day, unlike the quieter residential quarters

questions.instruction: Gap (8): divided into districts, ______ all the purveyors of one type grouped together
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITH. 'with all the purveyors grouped together' = với tất cả các nhà cung cấp được nhóm lại. Cấu trúc 'with + noun + past participle' (absolute construction) dùng để bổ sung thông tin. Chuyên đề: Grammar — with + noun + past participle.
questions.answerLocation: with all the purveyors of one type grouped together

questions.instruction: Gap (9): ______ the bazaar in smaller towns is..., in larger cities it is...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHILE (hoặc WHEREAS/WHILST). 'While the bazaar in smaller towns is... in larger cities it is...' = Trong khi chợ ở các thị trấn nhỏ là... ở các thành phố lớn hơn nó lại... Liên từ chỉ sự đối lập/tương phản. Chuyên đề: Conjunctions — while/whereas (contrast). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: While the bazaar in smaller towns is

questions.instruction: Gap (10): the bazaar in smaller towns is ______ of a single narrow street
questions.explanation: Đáp án: THAT. 'is that of a single narrow street' = là cái của một con đường hẹp duy nhất. 'That' ở đây thay thế cho 'the bazaar' để tránh lặp từ. 'That of' = cái/loại của. Chuyên đề: Reference words — that of. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: is that of a single narrow street

questions.instruction: Gap (11): it is by ______ means simple
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NO. 'by no means' = hoàn toàn không, tuyệt nhiên không. 'It is by no means simple' = nó hoàn toàn không đơn giản. Đây là fixed expression rất phổ biến ở trình độ CPE. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — by no means. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: it is by no means simple

questions.instruction: Gap (12): such as the ______ built at Sashan and Isfahan
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ONES. 'the ones built at' = những cái được xây dựng tại. 'Ones' thay thế cho 'bazaars' để tránh lặp từ. 'The ones built at Sashan and Isfahan' = những chợ được xây tại Sashan và Isfahan. Chuyên đề: Reference words — ones. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: the ones built at Sashan and Isfahan

questions.instruction: Gap (13): roofed for protection ______ the hot desert sun
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FROM (hoặc AGAINST). 'protection from/against' = sự bảo vệ khỏi. 'Roofed for protection from the hot desert sun' = được lợp mái để bảo vệ khỏi nắng sa mạc nóng. Cả 'from' và 'against' đều đi với 'protection'. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — protection from/against.
questions.answerLocation: protection from the hot desert sun

questions.instruction: Gap (14): ______ with a single roof of individual vaulted domes or with awnings
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EITHER. 'either with a single roof... or with awnings' = hoặc bằng một mái đơn... hoặc bằng mái hiên. Cấu trúc 'either...or' để trình bày hai phương án. Chuyên đề: Conjunctions — either...or.
questions.answerLocation: either with a single roof or with awnings

questions.instruction: Gap (15): have gradually been modernized ______ the centuries
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OVER (hoặc THROUGH). 'over/through the centuries' = qua nhiều thế kỷ. 'Modernized over the centuries' = được hiện đại hóa qua nhiều thế kỷ. Cả 'over' và 'through' đều chấp nhận được khi nói về khoảng thời gian dài. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — over/through time.
questions.answerLocation: modernized over the centuries

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 16-25, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
The Cat

The (0) domesticated cat, more commonly referred to as the house cat, (DOMESTIC) is the smallest member of the (16) ______ feline family. (EXTEND)

Like their wild cousins, house cats (17) ______ have streamlined bodies, classically shaped skulls, elongated tails and specially evolved teeth and claws. (CHARACTER)

All of these physical attributes contribute to the (18) ______ of the cat as a hunter. Cats, both domestic and (AGILE) wild, are renowned for their acute sense of balance, amazing (19) ______ and lithe, (20) ______ movements. (GRACE / GRACE)

In contrast to man's best friend the dog, cats are not considered to be social animals in the sense that they have never (21) ______ travelled in packs or adopted leaders. Dogs, on the other hand, which have always been social (22) ______, seem to have been better suited for the fireside hearth as they (CREATE)

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct word form.

questions.instruction: Gap (16): the smallest member of the ______ feline family (EXTEND)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EXTENDED (từ gốc: EXTEND). Thêm hậu tố '-ed' để tạo tính từ. 'The extended feline family' = gia đình mèo mở rộng. 'Extended family' là collocation chuẩn chỉ đại gia đình (bao gồm cả họ hàng xa). Cần tính từ vì đứng trước danh từ 'feline family'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-ed from verbs).
questions.answerLocation: the extended feline family

questions.instruction: Gap (17): house cats ______ have streamlined bodies (CHARACTER)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CHARACTERISTICALLY (từ gốc: CHARACTER). Thêm hậu tố '-istic' rồi '-ally' để tạo trạng từ. 'Characteristically have streamlined bodies' = theo đặc điểm đặc trưng, có thân hình khí động học. Cần trạng từ vì bổ nghĩa cho động từ 'have'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adverbs (-ally).
questions.answerLocation: characteristically have streamlined bodies

questions.instruction: Gap (18): contribute to the ______ of the cat as a hunter (AGILE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AGILITY (từ gốc: AGILE). Thêm hậu tố '-ity' để tạo danh từ. 'The agility of the cat' = sự nhanh nhẹn của mèo. Cần danh từ vì đứng sau mạo từ 'the' và giới từ 'to the'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ity).
questions.answerLocation: the agility of the cat as a hunter

questions.instruction: Gap (19): amazing ______ (GRACE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: GRACEFULNESS (từ gốc: GRACE). Thêm hậu tố '-ful' rồi '-ness' để tạo danh từ. 'Amazing gracefulness' = sự duyên dáng đáng kinh ngạc. Cần danh từ vì song song với 'balance' và bổ nghĩa bởi tính từ 'amazing'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-fulness).
questions.answerLocation: amazing gracefulness

questions.instruction: Gap (20): lithe, ______ movements (GRACE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: GRACEFUL (từ gốc: GRACE). Thêm hậu tố '-ful' để tạo tính từ. 'Graceful movements' = những chuyển động duyên dáng. Cần tính từ vì đứng trước danh từ 'movements' và song song với 'lithe'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-ful).
questions.answerLocation: lithe, graceful movements

questions.instruction: Gap (21): they have never ______ travelled in packs (CUSTOM)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CUSTOMARILY (từ gốc: CUSTOM). Thêm hậu tố '-ary' rồi '-ily' để tạo trạng từ. 'Never customarily travelled in packs' = chưa bao giờ thường xuyên di chuyển theo bầy. Cần trạng từ vì bổ nghĩa cho động từ 'travelled'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adverbs (-arily).
questions.answerLocation: never customarily travelled in packs

questions.instruction: Gap (22): have always been social ______ (CREATE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CREATURES (từ gốc: CREATE). Chuyển đổi thành 'creatures' = sinh vật, tạo vật. 'Social creatures' = sinh vật xã hội. Cần danh từ số nhiều vì 'dogs' là số nhiều và cấu trúc 'been + noun'. Lưu ý: 'creature' bắt nguồn từ 'create' (tạo ra → tạo vật). Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (root transformation).
questions.answerLocation: social creatures

questions.instruction: Gap (23): (word formation question 23 from text)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OBEDIENTLY (từ gốc: OBEY). Thêm hậu tố '-ient' rồi '-ly' để tạo trạng từ. Mèo không vâng lời như chó — chó 'obediently' tuân theo mệnh lệnh. Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho hành vi của chó. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adverbs (-iently).
questions.answerLocation: obediently

questions.instruction: Gap (24): (word formation question 24 from text)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DEPENDENCE (từ gốc: DEPEND). Thêm hậu tố '-ence' để tạo danh từ. 'Dependence' = sự phụ thuộc, dùng trong ngữ cảnh nói về mối quan hệ giữa chó và người. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ence).
questions.answerLocation: dependence

questions.instruction: Gap (25): (word formation question 25 from text)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: INDEPENDENCE (từ gốc: DEPEND). Thêm tiền tố 'in-' và hậu tố '-ence' để tạo danh từ phủ định. 'Independence' = sự độc lập — mèo nổi tiếng với tính độc lập. Bẫy: 'dependence' (không có tiền tố phủ định) sẽ đúng cho chó nhưng sai cho mèo. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (in- + -ence).
questions.answerLocation: independence

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 32-39, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

32. The book was not as good as I had expected, considering it had been written by such a good novelist. (LIVED)

The book ______ it had been written by such a good novelist.

33. I am sure that they didn't complete the work on time. (COULD)

They ______ the work on time.

34. His promotion meant that he could no longer take part in the weekly meeting. (RULED)

His promotion ______ part in the weekly meeting.

35. They say that the company is losing money. (RUMOURED)

The company ______ money.

36. If anyone can get lost, he will. (DEPENDED)

He ______ get lost.

37. Philip rarely bothers about what other people think. (TAKES)

Philip ______ what other people think.

38. Protective clothing must be worn when entering this area. (FORBIDDEN)

It ______ protective clothing.

39. There will be no progress until we have fully understood the problem. (FULL)

Not until there is ______ be any progress.

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gap for each sentence below using 3 to 8 words.

questions.instruction: Sentence 32: The book ______ it had been written by such a good novelist. (Key word: LIVED)
acceptedAnswers: didn't live up to expectations considering
did not live up to my expectations considering
questions.correctAnswer: didn't live up to expectations considering
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'didn't live up to expectations considering'. 'Live up to expectations' = đáp ứng kỳ vọng. 'The book didn't live up to expectations considering it had been written by such a good novelist' = Cuốn sách không đáp ứng kỳ vọng xét rằng nó được viết bởi một tiểu thuyết gia giỏi. Bẫy: 'lived up' cần 'to' phía sau — 'live up to'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — live up to. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: didn't live up to expectations considering

questions.instruction: Sentence 33: They ______ the work on time. (Key word: COULD)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'couldn't have completed'. 'couldn't have + past participle' = chắc chắn không thể đã... (suy luận về quá khứ). 'They couldn't have completed the work on time' = Họ chắc chắn không thể đã hoàn thành công việc đúng hạn. Đây là modal verb for deduction in the past. Chuyên đề: Modal verbs — couldn't have + past participle. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: couldn't have completed the work on time

questions.instruction: Sentence 34: His promotion ______ part in the weekly meeting. (Key word: RULED)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'ruled out his taking'. 'Rule out' = loại trừ, ngăn cản. 'His promotion ruled out his taking part' = Sự thăng chức đã khiến anh ta không thể tham gia. Phrasal verb 'rule out' + gerund. Bẫy: 'ruled him out of taking' cũng chấp nhận được. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — rule out. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: ruled out his taking part

questions.instruction: Sentence 35: The company ______ money. (Key word: RUMOURED)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'is rumoured to be losing'. 'be rumoured to' = được đồn đại là. Cấu trúc bị động với 'rumour': 'The company is rumoured to be losing money' = Công ty được đồn đại là đang thua lỗ. Bẫy: cần 'to be losing' (continuous) vì hành động đang diễn ra. Chuyên đề: Passive reporting structures — be rumoured to.
questions.answerLocation: is rumoured to be losing money

questions.instruction: Sentence 36: He ______ get lost. (Key word: DEPENDED)
acceptedAnswers: can always be depended upon to
can always be depended on to
questions.correctAnswer: can always be depended upon to
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'can always be depended upon to'. 'be depended upon/on to' = có thể tin chắc rằng sẽ. 'He can always be depended upon to get lost' = Anh ta luôn có thể được tin chắc là sẽ đi lạc (= nếu ai có thể lạc đường, thì chắc chắn là anh ta). Bẫy: cần 'upon/on' sau 'depended'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — depend upon/on (passive).
questions.answerLocation: can always be depended upon to get lost

questions.instruction: Sentence 37: Philip ______ what other people think. (Key word: TAKES)
acceptedAnswers: hardly ever takes any notice of
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'rarely takes any notice of'. 'take notice of' = chú ý đến, quan tâm đến. 'Philip rarely takes any notice of what other people think' = Philip hiếm khi quan tâm đến ý kiến người khác. Câu gốc 'rarely bothers about' ≈ 'rarely takes any notice of'. Chuyên đề: Collocations — take notice of.
questions.answerLocation: rarely takes any notice of what other people think

questions.instruction: Sentence 38: It ______ protective clothing. (Key word: FORBIDDEN)
acceptedAnswers: is forbidden to enter this area without
is forbidden to enter this area without wearing
questions.correctAnswer: is forbidden to enter this area without
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'is forbidden to enter this area without'. 'It is forbidden to' = bị cấm. 'It is forbidden to enter this area without protective clothing' = Bị cấm vào khu vực này mà không có quần áo bảo hộ. Câu gốc 'must be worn' chuyển thành 'forbidden to enter without'. Chuyên đề: Passive structures — it is forbidden to. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: is forbidden to enter this area without protective clothing

questions.instruction: Sentence 39: Not until there is ______ be any progress. (Key word: FULL)
acceptedAnswers: a full understanding of the problem will there
full understanding of the problem will there
questions.correctAnswer: a full understanding of the problem will there
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'a full understanding of the problem will there'. 'Full understanding' dùng key word 'FULL'. Cấu trúc đảo ngữ: 'Not until there is X will there be Y'. 'Not until there is a full understanding of the problem will there be any progress' = Cho đến khi hiểu rõ hoàn toàn vấn đề thì mới có tiến triển. Bẫy: cần đảo ngữ 'will there' sau 'not until'. Chuyên đề: Inversion — not until.
questions.answerLocation: full understanding of the problem will there be any progress

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read four extracts about children and their development, and an article about lenses used in painting. For questions 19-26 and 34-40, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Fears and Tears

Like many parents I had not fully appreciated the emotional upheaval going to school for the first time involves for a child. I had tended to focus on my own feelings, and notwithstanding my awareness that this was a major step in my daughter's life, my own reluctant acceptance of this as a rite of passage which signalled the end of babyhood, had preoccupied me to the exclusion of all else. Never once did I imagine she would have any objections.

To be fair to myself, this was partly because, having gone back to work relatively soon after she was born, I had had to leave her at home in the care of a childminder from a very early age, so she was quite a sociable child. What I had failed to appreciate, though, was the strong sense of place young children possess. To my daughter, what mattered, perhaps more than anything else, was the shift to a new, possibly threatening environment.

Perhaps it is impossible for adults, with our acceptance of the transience of everything in the modern world, to understand how primitive small children still are in their reactions to the world around them. The world is unchanging, permanent and any alteration represents an enormous tragedy. At any rate, my attempts to leave her at nursery school were greeted with heart-rending screams which left me shaken and shamefaced as I slunk away to my office.

Early Literacy Development

This great resource gives the latest information on emerging reading and writing skills. You will get facts and background information to help you identify the stages of literacy development as well as strategies to facilitate them for future academic success. Learn about the characteristics and factors that promote or inhibit reading acquisition and writing development. This resource covers: stages in early reading and writing; characteristics of delay and 'at risk' factors; reading and writing facilitation strategies; literacy activities; suggestions for working with parents and families; lists of picture books.

Growing Up

My friends were quite envious of my having famous parents. That set me apart in a strange way. At other times though, I could be terribly embarrassed by the fact that my parents weren't the same as everyone else's parents. I think that when you are growing up you want to fit into some pattern, but you don't see a big enough picture to know what that pattern really is. You just see a very narrow social pattern into which you have been put and if you are spilling out over that in some way, it can be very embarrassing for a child.

I would love childhood to mean a sort of free growing, but in reality it never is because all children, no matter what their background, have dark corners which they keep to themselves. There is never that openness — it's just the nature of the beasts, both parents and children. The one cannot actually see life from the perspective of the other, and so the 'dark corners' become caverns of misunderstanding at times. It's only when you're grown up that you can actually come to terms with those misunderstandings and see your parents as they really are.

Post-Traumatic Stress

Child maltreatment is a major health problem in the United States, with, according to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, more than 1 million cases of child abuse documented in 1994. It is well established that exposure to trauma greatly increases an individual's long-term vulnerability to psychiatric problems. Besides being linked to the development of post-traumatic stress disorder, it has been found that a high level of adult patients suffering from major depression, panic disorder and personality disorder were abused, either physically or psychologically, as children. Clarifying what follows from trauma and classifying the various types of abuse may provide pertinent information which could lead to better means of treatment for those individuals who were maltreated as children.

Lenses in Painting

David Hockney's breakthrough work on the use made by the old masters of mirrors and lenses is becoming well known. Though there are carpers and hecklers, it has leapt from hunch, through theory, to accepted fact, in a remarkably short time. His basic idea, that the use of lenses — effectively photography without the chemical fixing — spread throughout European painting from the Renaissance onwards, alters forever our own perception of some of the greatest artists in history.

Two years ago, Hockney was at the National Gallery's Ingres show in London. He was gripped with curiosity at the speed, accuracy and odd certainties of Ingres' extraordinarily fast portrait sketches done in Rome and thought Ingres might have used an optical device called a camera lucida. Hockney obtained one and, with its help, was soon also doing very fast, accurate pencil portraits. The more paintings Hockney studied, the more evidence he found that lenses were used. This is not to say that the great names in Western art were cheating. Why is using a lens cheating, any more than using a plumb line? But they were certainly being helped... with photography.

Hockney created a massive gallery of photos and postcards of paintings on a wall of his Californian studio, running from the 1300s to the last century, divided into Northern and Southern European, along which breakthroughs in realism become strikingly obvious. Each, he thinks, corresponds to a new technological breakthrough in lens making, with the first around 1420.

Lenses show movement; the projections, hundreds of years ago, are far better than high-definition television. In clarity and colour, they are amazing, but they could not be preserved, except by drawing. They were living projections. There is no doubt that painters saw colour images, optical projections which look like paintings, and they made paintings which look like optical projections. The old masters saw moving colour pictures.

Hockney pursued the theory with art historians and friends. From everywhere, the evidence accumulated. A mere artist, a hand, brush and pencil man, seemed to have spotted what the combined intellects of academic art history had missed.

The computer allows the manipulation of the photographic image. Chemical photography gives way to digital technology, which can be infinitely manipulated and manipulation means drawing. Since photographs are going to be increasingly made by drawing, the photograph has really lost its veracity.

Here, triumphantly, the Hockney case that the great masters used lenses comes full circle, returning us in 2001 to the revival of drawing, another life-long passion. 'For 400, nearly 500 years, the hand was involved with the camera. Artists were using the lens. Then for 160 years, you had chemical photography. But that has now come to an end, and with digital photography, you have got the hand back in the camera.'

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

questions.instruction: 19. What had the writer not expected her child to do on the first day of school?
options: B. Feel comfortable in new surroundings.
C. Get on with the other children.
D. Rebel at her new situation.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Câu 'Never once did I imagine she would have any objections' = Tôi chưa bao giờ tưởng tượng cô bé sẽ phản đối. 'Rebel at her new situation' = nổi loạn/phản đối tình huống mới. Mẹ không ngờ con gái sẽ chống đối việc đi học. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn nhưng câu hỏi hỏi điều mẹ KHÔNG NGỜTỚI, không phải điều mẹ mong đợi. B nói về cảm giác thoải mái nhưng vấn đề chính là sự phản đối. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: Never once did I imagine she would have any objections

questions.instruction: 20. How does the writer seem to have felt when she left her daughter at nursery?
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu cuối: 'left me shaken and shamefaced as I slunk away to my office' — 'shamefaced' = xấu hổ, mang cảm giác tội lỗi. 'Slunk away' = lẻn đi một cách xấu hổ. Những từ này thể hiện cảm giác tội lỗi (guilty). Bẫy: B (relieved = nhẹ nhõm) hoàn toàn ngược ý. C (startled = giật mình) quá nhẹ. D (unconcerned = thờ ơ) ngược ý. Chuyên đề: Reading — attitude & vocabulary.
questions.answerLocation: left me shaken and shamefaced as I slunk away

questions.instruction: 21. What does the resource suggest about early reading and writing skills?
options: A. They are now appearing at a later stage.
B. They slow later progress at school.
C. They are not always easy to acquire.
D. Parents should not be involved in developing them.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Bài nói về 'factors that promote or inhibit reading acquisition' và 'characteristics of delay and at risk factors' — tức là có những yếu tố cản trở và rủi ro. Điều này chứng tỏ 'they are not always easy to acquire' = không phải lúc nào cũng dễ dàng tiếp thu. Bẫy: D sai hoàn toàn vì bài bao gồm 'suggestions for working with parents and families'. A và B không có bằng chứng trong bài. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: factors that promote or inhibit reading acquisition... characteristics of delay

questions.instruction: 22. Who is the resource primarily aimed at?
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Mặc dù bài đề cập 'working with parents', nội dung chủ yếu dành cho giáo viên/nhà giáo dục: 'identify the stages of literacy development', 'strategies to facilitate them', 'reading and writing facilitation strategies'. Đây là ngôn ngữ chuyên môn dành cho người dạy. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn vì có đề cập 'parents' nhưng đó chỉ là một phần nhỏ. Chuyên đề: Reading — purpose & audience.
questions.answerLocation: strategies to facilitate them... facilitation strategies

questions.instruction: 23. In the first paragraph, the writer implies that children are usually
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'when you are growing up you want to fit into some pattern' và 'if you are spilling out over that in some way, it can be very embarrassing'. Trẻ em muốn HÒA NHẬP (fit in), tuân theo khuôn mẫu xã hội → 'conformists' = người theo khuôn mẫu. Bẫy: A không đúng — tác giả nói CÓ LÚC xấu hổ vì bố mẹ khác biệt, nhưng đó không phải ý chính. B không có bằng chứng. D ngược ý. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference & implication.
questions.answerLocation: you want to fit into some pattern... spilling out can be very embarrassing

questions.instruction: 24. According to the writer, children
options: A. are in need of parental protection.
B. cannot regard their parents as individuals.
C. need to be open with their parents.
D. have high expectations of their parents.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'The one cannot actually see life from the perspective of the other' và 'It's only when you're grown up that you can actually come to terms with those misunderstandings and see your parents as they really are.' — Trẻ em KHÔNG THỂ nhìn bố mẹ như những cá nhân thực sự cho đến khi trưởng thành. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn vì có đề cập 'protect from the world' nhưng có '(probably quite wrongly)' — tác giả phủ nhận điều này. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail & opinion.
questions.answerLocation: It's only when you're grown up that you can see your parents as they really are

questions.instruction: 25. Children who are abused early in life
options: A. can no longer be properly helped.
B. are likely to have mental problems in later life.
C. will all need psychiatric help when they grow up.
D. will be depressed a great deal of the time.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'exposure to trauma greatly increases an individual's long-term vulnerability to psychiatric problems' = tiếp xúc với chấn thương làm TĂNG nguy cơ (vulnerability) bị vấn đề tâm thần lâu dài. 'Are likely to' = có khả năng. Bẫy: A sai vì bài nói về 'better means of treatment' — tức là vẫn có thể giúp. C quá tuyệt đối ('all'). D quá cụ thể ('depressed' chỉ là một trong nhiều vấn đề). Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: exposure to trauma greatly increases vulnerability to psychiatric problems

questions.instruction: 26. Treatment is most likely to be helped
options: A. when the victim has reached adulthood.
B. after it is learned how the child suffered.
C. by giving the victim certain facts.
D. by the sufferer's personality type.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu cuối: 'Clarifying what follows from trauma and classifying the various types of abuse may provide pertinent information which could lead to better means of treatment' = Làm rõ hậu quả và phân loại các hình thức bạo lực có thể dẫn đến phương pháp điều trị tốt hơn. 'After it is learned how the child suffered' = sau khi biết được đứa trẻ đã chịu đựng thế nào. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: Clarifying what follows from trauma and classifying the types of abuse

questions.instruction: 34. The result of David Hockney's work is that
options: A. the theory has been accepted too quickly.
B. it is no longer understood how these artists painted.
C. the work of well-known artists has been discredited.
D. the artists' works must be viewed differently.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Câu then chốt: 'alters forever our own perception of some of the greatest artists in history' = thay đổi vĩnh viễn nhận thức của chúng ta về những nghệ sĩ vĩ đại nhất. 'Must be viewed differently' = phải được nhìn nhận khác đi. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn vì bài nói 'leapt to accepted fact in a remarkably short time' nhưng đó là mô tả, không phải KẾT QUẢ chính. C sai vì bài nói lõ 'This is not to say they were cheating'. Chuyên đề: Reading — main idea.
questions.answerLocation: alters forever our own perception of some of the greatest artists

questions.instruction: 35. The writer takes pains not to suggest that the painters were cheating because
options: A. Hockney could do fast, accurate pencil drawings using lenses.
B. he considers the use of lenses an acceptable technique.
C. as the paintings were studied, it became clear that lenses were used.
D. modern day artists use photography to help them.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'This is not to say that the great names in Western art were cheating. Why is using a lens cheating, any more than using a plumb line?' — Tác giả lập luận rằng dùng thấu kính không phải gian lận, tương tự như dùng dây dọi. Tức là tác giả coi đó là KỸ THUẬT CHẤP NHẬN ĐƯỢC (acceptable technique). Bẫy: A, C, D đều là sự thật nhưng không phải LÝ DO tác giả tránh gọi đó là gian lận. Chuyên đề: Reading — writer's purpose.
questions.answerLocation: Why is using a lens cheating, any more than using a plumb line?

questions.instruction: 36. Hockney considers that drawing became better in 1420 because
options: A. the improvement corresponded with the first improvement in lensmaking.
B. the depiction of objects suddenly gained greater importance in art.
C. each breakthrough in realism corresponded with a breakthrough in lens making.
D. new types of lenses spread across Italy from then onwards.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu then chốt: 'Each, he thinks, corresponds to a new technological breakthrough in lens making, with the first around 1420.' — Sự cải thiện ĐẦU TIÊN tương ứng với bước đột phá đầu tiên trong chế tạo thấu kính KHOẢNG năm 1420. Bẫy: C hấp dẫn vì cũng nói về sự tương ứng, nhưng câu hỏi hỏi CỤ THỂ về năm 1420 (bước đột phá ĐẦU TIÊN), không phải tất cả. D chỉ đúng một phần và không phải lý do chính. Chuyên đề: Reading — specific detail.
questions.answerLocation: corresponds to a new technological breakthrough in lens making, with the first around 1420

questions.instruction: 37. The old masters used lenses to
options: B. keep a record of the image.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'they could not be preserved, except by drawing. They were living projections.' — Hình ảnh từ thấu kính không thể bảo quản, trừ khi vẽ lại. Họ dùng thấu kính để GHI LẠI hình ảnh (keep a record) bằng cách vẽ. Bẫy: C đúng về mặt kỹ thuật (họ thấy hình màu) nhưng đó không phải MỤC ĐÍCH sử dụng. D hấp dẫn nhưng bài nói họ dùng thấu kính để VẼ, không phải CẢI THIỆN khả năng. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: they could not be preserved, except by drawing

questions.instruction: 38. How did artists react when fixed photography was introduced?
options: A. They attempted to paint their subjects more realistically.
B. They responded by using lenses themselves to paint.
C. They turned to a more abstract style.
D. They began using computers instead of a camera.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'Artists respond by either trying to turn back to the pre-lens era or by emphasising an anti-photographic style of painting' — Họ phản ứng bằng cách quay về thời kỳ trước thấu kính HOẶC nhấn mạnh phong cách hội họa phi nhiếp ảnh (anti-photographic) = trừu tượng hơn. Bẫy: A ngược ý (họ làm NGƯỢC LẠI — bớt thực tế hơn). B không phù hợp — nhiếp ảnh đe dọa họa sĩ. D thuộc giai đoạn thứ ba, không phải phản ứng với nhiếp ảnh cố định. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: C. They turned to a more abstract style.

questions.instruction: 39. What does the writer mean when he says that photography has 'lost its veracity'?
options: A. Digital technology is increasingly being used in photography.
B. Artists are using computers to produce their work instead of brushes.
C. Photographs cannot be relied upon to produce a true image.
D. Digital technology allows images to be changed by hand.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'Veracity' = tính xác thực/chân thực. 'Lost its veracity' = mất đi tính xác thực. Câu trước: 'photographs are going to be increasingly made by drawing' — ảnh ngày càng được tạo bằng vẽ (chỉnh sửa), nên không còn đáng tin về tính chân thực. 'Cannot be relied upon to produce a true image' = không thể tin cậy để tạo ra hình ảnh thực. Bẫy: A và D đúng nhưng chỉ nói về nguyên nhân, không phải ý NGHĨA của 'lost its veracity'. Chuyên đề: Reading — vocabulary in context.
questions.answerLocation: the photograph has really lost its veracity

questions.instruction: 40. What does the writer mean in paragraph 7 by 'the Hockney case... comes full circle'?
options: A. Artists are reverting to an older style of painting.
B. Photography now involves earlier techniques.
C. Artists are increasingly using lenses again.
D. Painting and drawing have been superseded by photography.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'Comes full circle' = quay trở lại điểm xuất phát. Câu then chốt: 'with digital photography, you have got the hand back in the camera' — với nhiếp ảnh kỹ thuật số, bàn tay (vẽ) lại quay về với máy ảnh. Tức là nhiếp ảnh hiện đại lại SỬ DỤNG kỹ thuật trước đây (vẽ tay + máy ảnh). Bẫy: A nói về hội họa, nhưng ý chính là NHIẾP ẢNH quay lại kỹ thuật cũ. C sai vì không phải dùng thấu kính lại. D ngược ý. Chuyên đề: Reading — idiom in context.
questions.answerLocation: with digital photography, you have got the hand back in the camera

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about the Spanish treasure fleets. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (27-33). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Spanish Treasure Fleets

Gold earrings hung with pearls sank with a Spanish galleon west of Havana, one of the many wrecked by pirates, storms and treacherous reefs. These and other artefacts offer a wealth of clues about the history of Cuba's golden past. A glittering fortune in gold and silver has been recovered from the sea floor. Treasures including luxuries such as rare wood and exotic feathers were shipped from the New World to Seville by way of Cuba.

[Gap 27]

In a typical year, the first of the two annual treasure fleets left Spain in spring and entered the Caribbean near the island of Margarita, off Venezuela — a source of pearls and a frequent target of pirates. Here the flotilla usually split in two, following courses that touched much of the Spanish New World. One convoy stopped at ports along the Spanish Main, as the English called the northern coast of South America and the Caribbean islands. Colonists, forbidden to manufacture anything, had to buy even such ordinary items as cutlery, tools and religious medals from the convoy.

[Gap 28]

In late summer, the merchant ships and war ships sailed to Havana's well-fortified harbour to form the treasure fleet. Theoretically, the captain general and his warships defended all the merchantmen against pirates. In reality, storms frequently scattered the flotilla making individual ships vulnerable. Pirates chose these loners to attack and loot. But Piet Heyn, to the Spanish a pirate, to the Dutch a fabled admiral, was not satisfied with picking off the stragglers. He wanted the whole treasure.

[Gap 29]

Officials in Havana, who feared this legendary figure more than any other foe, kept watch for him, especially when a treasure fleet was about to sail for Spain. On August 4, 1628, Heyn and his ships lay off Cuba, not sure whether the treasure fleet's Mexican component had left for Havana. Spanish scout vessels spotted the Dutch and sent swift courier ships to Veracruz to warn Juan de Benavides, captain general of the treasure fleet. But, unknown to the Spanish, Heyn had captured one of the courier ships.

[Gap 30]

Finally in August, he set sail again. As he neared Matanzas Bay, about 50 miles east of Havana, he saw more than 30 Dutch warships bearing down on him. 'I continued my course, resolved to die,' Benavides bravely wrote in a letter to the king. But another officer later testified that Benavides had foolishly led the fleet into the bay. In his panic, he grounded his own ship and all that followed.

[Gap 31]

'I jumped into a boat,' Benavides later recounted, claiming he had arranged in vain for his ship to be set afire in his absence. Leoz, seeing his ship boarded by the Dutchmen, ran below, changed into the clothes of an ordinary sailor, and slipped in among the crewmen who already had laid down their muskets.

[Gap 32]

That done, Heyn put his men aboard the six looted galleons, along with three others, and sent them off to the Netherlands. Benavides' flagship, so jammed with cargo that the cannon ports were obstructed, had 29 guns; Leoz's had 22. Neither had fired a shot.

[Gap 33]

The story of Heyn's triumph and Benavides' death is preserved in the General Archives of the Indies in Seville, Spain. Treasure searchers begin here, sifting through the voluminous records that officials kept on every flotilla, on every ship and every cargo.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph you do not need to use.

questions.instruction: Gap 27:
questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Đoạn F giới thiệu về tàu kho báu: 'Hundreds of ships sank in Cuban waters... ships of the Spanish treasure fleets, the flotillas which carried New World gold, silver and gems... Casa de Contratacion ordered merchant ships to travel in convoy, guarded by armed warships.' Đoạn này là phần giới thiệu tổng quan về đội tàu kho báu, nối tiếp phần mô tả những hiện vật được tìm thấy ở đoạn trước, và dẫn đến đoạn tiếp theo mô tả lộ trình cụ thể. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — introduction & topic development.
questions.answerLocation: ships of the Spanish treasure fleets

questions.instruction: Gap 28:
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn C nói về đội tàu khác đi đến Cartagena và Portobelo — điểm thu gom bạc từ Peru. 'Other ships carrying similar cargoes sailed into Cartagena, Colombia, and then west to Portobelo, Panama, the collecting point for the silver.' Đây là phần tiếp nối mô tả lộ trình của nửa đội tàu còn lại sau khi chia tách. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — parallel information.
questions.answerLocation: Other ships carrying similar cargoes sailed into Cartagena

questions.instruction: Gap 29:
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D giới thiệu lý lịch của Piet Heyn: 'As a young privateer in Spanish waters, he had been captured and sentenced to be a galley slave. Freed in a prisoner exchange, he returned to sea and sought vengeance.' Đoạn trước vừa giới thiệu Heyn muốn toàn bộ kho báu — đoạn này giải thích LÝ DO (động cơ trả thù). Chuyên đề: Gapped text — character background.
questions.answerLocation: As a young privateer... he returned to sea and sought vengeance

questions.instruction: Gap 30:
questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G giới thiệu Benavides: 'Scion of a wealthy family of shipbuilders, Juan de Benavides was an admiral who had never fought a sea battle. He got his appointment through influence, not skill.' Tiếp theo mô tả ông rời Veracruz nhưng phải quay lại vì 'emergency'. Nối trước đoạn 'Finally in August, he set sail again'. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — character introduction.
questions.answerLocation: Benavides was an admiral who had never fought a sea battle

questions.instruction: Gap 31:
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A mô tả việc tàu Hà Lan truy đuổi: 'Their pursuers rapidly closed in, anchored or grounded their ships, boarded boats manned with musketeers and headed for the hapless Spanish ships. The Dutch swarmed aboard Benavides' ship and the ship of Admiral Don Juan de Leoz.' Nối tiếp đoạn Benavides mắc cạn tàu, dẫn đến đoạn Benavides nhảy xuống thuyền nhỏ. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — action sequence.
questions.answerLocation: The Dutch swarmed aboard Benavides' ship

questions.instruction: Gap 32:
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E mô tả kết quả cướp: 'Flushed with a previous success — they had already captured nine ships of the silver fleet — Heyn and his men seized half a dozen Spanish ships and put the Spaniards ashore. In the days that followed, the Dutch sailors inventoried and transferred the plunder, which included 46 tons of silver.' Nối tiếp việc Leoz trốn thoát, dẫn đến 'That done' ở đoạn sau. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — result & consequence.
questions.answerLocation: Heyn and his men seized half a dozen Spanish ships

questions.instruction: Gap 33:
questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn H mô tả hậu quả: 'The Netherlands hailed Heyn as a hero... Benavides was tried, not for loss of the treasure fleet but for cowardice, and later executed. Heyn... was killed by a cannonball.' Đoạn trước nói không tàu nào bắn một phát, đoạn sau nói câu chuyện được lưu giữ trong lưu trữ. H là phần kết thúc câu chuyện trước phần epilogue. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — aftermath.
questions.answerLocation: Benavides was tried for cowardice and later executed

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: The Mediterranean Basin is one example of this and in recent decades decreasing trends in precipitation totals have already been identified in western-central parts of the basin as well as marked changes in seasonality. A clear tendency for rainfall to be concentrated into a shorter period of the year has been noted in the Alentejo region of southern Portugal, with the proportion of annual rainfall falling in autumn and winter, increasing at the expense of spring totals.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: Unfortunately, they are simplifications of the real world and have numerous deficiencies. Their results are only approximate and they are also slow to run and expensive to use. Part of the problem is that we do not understand fully all the processes of the climatic system, although we do realise its complexity.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: Increased flooding and inundation are the most obvious results, with London, New York and Tokyo being just a few of the candidates for significant disruption. Huge numbers of people stand to lose their homes and livelihoods and this could produce many millions of environmental refugees.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: But this would not be true for all. In fact, some climate change impacts will probably be beneficial. Scientists in some countries have already identified useful environmental trends that are closely linked to the warming that has occurred to date. In Australia, for example, research has shown that the average yield of wheat has increased by about 0.5 tonnes per hectare since 1952, and climate trends have played a significant part in this greater food production.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: This would indicate that the two are almost certainly related. Evidence gleaned from a range of sources suggests that our planet has warmed at the surface by about 0.6°C over the past century. Most scientists think that this trend is unlikely to be natural in origin and is, in part, a result of human pollution of the atmosphere.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: This proves that the earth's climate has never been static and human impact on climate has been relatively minor, compared to naturally occurring large-scale perturbations. Ice ages, for example, result from natural changes in the earth's orbit around the sun. But the most worrying type of human-induced climatic change has been brought about through modifications to the natural atmospheric mix of greenhouse gases.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: We humans have learned to use such natural resources to our advantage, enabling us to produce food, build great cities and support six billion members of the human race. Any changes to these resources have to be taken seriously. The problem we have is knowing just how the world will change, and what is causing these changes.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: Currently, they suggest that the average annual global surface temperature will increase by between 1°C and 3.5°C by the year 2100; that the average sea level around the world will rise by 15-95 cm; and that changes in the spatial and temporal patterns of precipitation will occur. Scientists also expect extreme weather conditions, such as heatwaves, floods and droughts, to become more frequent in some places.
